county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1807; original Orleans Territory parish; River Road plantation region
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and West Baton Rouge Parish Clerk of Court (Port Allen). Original Orleans Territory parish (1807). West bank of the Mississippi across from Baton Rouge. Sugar plantation parish with extensive enslaved persons records. Catholic church registers at St. John the Evangelist (Plaquemine — now in Iberville, but historically connected). West Baton Rouge Museum has local history and sugar industry exhibits. French and Spanish colonial records. Cross-reference with East Baton Rouge and Iberville for related families. Port Allen area records. Small but historically significant parish.

source_profiles.source_name: West Baton Rouge Parish Conveyance Records — Clerk of Court
source_profiles.repository: West Baton Rouge Parish Clerk of Court, Port Allen
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Conveyance (deed) books for West Baton Rouge Parish from 1807 forward; Louisiana uses civil law system — property transfers recorded as notarial acts

source_profiles.source_name: West Baton Rouge Parish Succession Records — Clerk of Court
source_profiles.repository: West Baton Rouge Parish Clerk of Court, Port Allen
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Succession records (Louisiana's civil law equivalent of probate) for West Baton Rouge Parish; includes inventories, family meetings (conseil de famille), and partition records naming heirs

source_profiles.source_name: West Baton Rouge Parish Marriage Records — Clerk of Court / Catholic Church Registers
source_profiles.repository: West Baton Rouge Parish Clerk of Court / Louisiana State Archives / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil marriage licenses and Catholic church marriage registers for West Baton Rouge Parish; church records often predate civil registration
source_profiles.survival_notes: Statewide vital records registration from 1914; earlier records at parish level and in church registers

source_profiles.source_name: West Baton Rouge Parish Catholic Church Sacramental Registers
source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of New Orleans / Diocese of Lafayette / Diocese of Baton Rouge / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Catholic sacramental registers (baptism, marriage, burial) for West Baton Rouge Parish; includes separate registers for enslaved persons and free people of color in colonial and antebellum periods

source_profiles.source_name: West Baton Rouge Parish Enslaved Persons Records — Clerk of Court / Louisiana State Archives
source_profiles.repository: West Baton Rouge Parish Clerk of Court / Louisiana State Archives / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Enslaved persons records for West Baton Rouge Parish including succession inventories listing enslaved persons by name, age, and value; Catholic church baptismal registers of enslaved persons; Code Noir records; bills of sale

source_profiles.source_name: West Baton Rouge Parish Colonial Land Grants and US Land Patents
source_profiles.repository: Louisiana State Archives / BLM General Land Office
source_profiles.coverage_notes: French and Spanish colonial land grants, US territorial confirmations, and General Land Office patents for West Baton Rouge Parish area; Louisiana State Land Office records

source_profiles.source_name: West Baton Rouge Parish Tax Assessment Rolls — Louisiana State Archives
source_profiles.repository: Louisiana State Archives, Baton Rouge
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Parish tax assessment rolls for West Baton Rouge Parish listing property owners, real estate, and (before 1865) enslaved persons; useful when census records are missing

county_metadata.parent_county: Carroll (divided into East and West Carroll)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1877; Reconstruction-era formation; North Louisiana uplands
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and West Carroll Parish Clerk of Court (Oak Grove). Formed 1877 when Carroll Parish was divided into East and West Carroll. Northeast Louisiana delta cotton country. Pre-1877 records in Carroll Parish (check both East and West clerks). Oak Grove area families. Anglo-American settlement predominates. Protestant church records (Baptist, Methodist) primary sources. West Carroll Parish Library has limited local history. Bayou Macon area. Cross-reference with East Carroll and Richland for related families. Named for Charles Carroll of Carrollton, last surviving signer of the Declaration of Independence.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1824; antebellum parish; River Road plantation region
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and West Feliciana Parish Clerk of Court (St. Francisville). Formed 1824 when Feliciana Parish was divided into East and West. Florida Parishes — part of Republic of West Florida (1810). St. Francisville is one of Louisiana's most beautiful antebellum towns. Major cotton plantation parish with large enslaved population. Rosedown Plantation State Historic Site has extensive plantation records. The Myrtles Plantation area. Catholic and Episcopal church records (Grace Episcopal, 1827). Audubon painted here (Oakley House). Cross-reference with East Feliciana and Pointe Coupee for related families.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Louisiana parish formed 1852; antebellum parish; North Louisiana uplands
county_metadata.research_tips: Louisiana State Archives and Winn Parish Clerk of Court (Winnfield). Formed 1852 from Natchitoches, Rapides, and Catahoula. Central Louisiana uplands — piney woods. Pre-1852 records split among parent parishes (Catahoula is a burned parish). Winnfield was the home of three Louisiana governors including Huey P. Long and Earl K. Long. Louisiana Political Museum and Hall of Fame (Winnfield). Winn Parish Library has local history. Protestant church records predominate. Timber industry heritage. Cross-reference with Natchitoches and Rapides for earlier records. Anti-secession sentiment during Civil War.
